title: AMD, Broadcom, and Nvidia join hyperscalers to define optical scale-up interconnect of the future for AI clusters Meta, Microsoft, and OpenAI to benefit as speeds eventually scale to 3.2 Tb/s
summary: AMD, Broadcom, and Nvidia collaborate with Meta, Microsoft, and OpenAI to develop a protocol-agnostic optical scale-up interconnect for AI clusters, enabling future speeds of 3.2 Tb/s.
insight: Faster AI compute speeds will revolutionize data-intensive applications like AI research and training.

title: RTX 5090 gets 12% gaming boost in Ubuntu 26.04, thanks to optimizations with Gnome 50 latest popular distro update brings significant performance uplift for Nvidia's top GPU
summary: Ubuntu 26.04 brings a 12% gaming performance boost to Nvidia's RTX 5090 GPU. This improvement is attributed to optimizations made with Gnome 50. The latest distro update shows significant performance uplift for the top GPU.
insight: Optimized Gnome 50 boosts Nvidia gaming performance significantly.

title: China firm Lisuan's homegrown 6nm G100 series GPUs announced with up to 12GB of VRAM LX 7G106 can play Cyberpunk 2077 and other popular Steam games, arrives June 18 in China
summary: Lisuan Tech's G100 series GPUs, with up to 12GB VRAM, will be released in China on June 18. The LX G7106 is a gaming SKU with purported RTX 4060-like performance. Professional GPUs with up to 24GB ECC VRAM are also available.
insight: China's Lisuan Tech enters the high-performance GPU market for gaming.

title: IBM and Lam's new partnership paves the way toward sub-1nm logic using High-NA EUV Albany lab to pioneer dry resist process integration
summary: IBM and Lam partnered to develop sub-1nm logic using EUV Albany lab. The partnership aims to integrate dry resist process for nanosheet and nanostack architectures. Backside power delivery validation is also a key focus.
insight: Advances in sub-1nm logic may lead to significant computing breakthroughs.

title: Apple s brand new M5 MacBook Air and MacBook Pro models are already discounted on Amazon shave $50 off nearly the entire 2026 lineup
summary: Amazon offers a $50 discount on most 2026 MacBook models, making them cheaper than buying directly from Apple.
insight: Savings on new MacBooks with early Amazon deals

title: Keychron Q16 HE 8K Review: All-ceramic, but not all good
summary: The Keychron Q16 HE 8K is an all-ceramic keyboard with 8K polling rate, but its premium feel is lacking.
insight: Price may not match the perceived value and quality.

title: Iran hacking group claims attack on med-tech company Stryker says over 200,000 devices have been wiped clean and over 50TB of data extracted
summary: Stryker, a Fortune 500 med-tech company, has been hit with a massive cyberattack. The attack allegedly wiped clean over 200,000 devices and extracted over 50TB of data. This has affected employees and operations globally.
insight: Massive cyberattack compromises sensitive medical data worldwide simultaneously

title: Save $800 on this MSI desktop with RTX 5070, 2TB SSD, and 32GB of RAM get an Intel Core Ultra 265K gaming rig for just $1,899
summary: Get a high-performance gaming PC with RTX 5070, 2TB SSD, and 32GB RAM for $1,899.
insight: High-end gaming PC at an affordable price point for enthusiasts.

title: Acer Swift 16 AI Review: A Premium contender with a curious twist
summary: Acer's Swift 16 AI offers a premium design with an OLED screen and Intel silicon, but its massive haptic touchpad is underwhelming. The laptop provides a great balance of performance and visuals, making it a strong contender. However, its touchpad may not be its standout feature.
insight: OLED screen and Intel silicon make it a top performer choice.

title: Qatar helium shutdown puts chip supply chain on a two-week clock SK hynix forced to diversify after 30% of global supply removed from the market
summary: QatarEnergy's helium production remains offline after drone strikes, affecting global supply. SK hynix, a major chip manufacturer, faces supply chain disruptions. The shutdown puts the chip supply chain on a two-week clock.
insight: Global chip supply at risk due to critical helium shortage.

title: Meta reveals four new MTIA chips built for AI inference to be released on a six-month cadence
summary: Meta releases four new MTIA chips for AI inference, developed with Broadcom. These chips will be deployed within the next two years on a six-month cadence. The partnership aims to enhance AI processing capabilities.
insight: Accelerating AI processing with enhanced chip technology for Meta's applications.

title: Retailer denies memory replacement due to 4x increase in DDR5 pricing, says price increase would equate to an 'upgrade' for the customer Australian retailer refuses to replace faulty Corsair kit
summary: Australian retailer denies memory replacement due to DDR5 price surge, cites 'upgrade' as reason.
insight: Warranty terms can be redefined by price fluctuations.

title: Discovery Drive An ESP32-S3-based azimuth/elevation rotator for satellite dishes and SDR antennas (Crowdfunding)
summary: The Discovery Drive is a plug-and-play ESP32-S3-based rotator for satellite dishes and SDR antennas, offering easy tracking of polar-orbiting satellites and amateur radio satellites. It features a fully weatherproof design with a motor torque of up to 12.25 Nm and supports antenna payloads up to 5 kg. The rotator can be controlled via its web UI and connected to 12V power and Wi-Fi.
insight: Low-cost, plug-and-play satellite tracking for hobbyists and researchers worldwide.

title: ST ST64UWB Cortex-M85 ultra-wideband SoC supports IEEE 802.15.4z and 802.15.4ab UWB standards, radar sensing
summary: STMicroelectronics' ST64UWB SoC family supports IEEE 802.15.4z and 802.15.4ab UWB standards for longer-range, more reliable positioning and secure interactions. It targets digital car keys, hands-free access, and smart-device detection. The SoC family includes various models with improved RF link budget.
insight: Enhanced proximity and positioning capabilities for automotive and smart home applications.

title: MediaTek unveils 50 TOPS Genio Pro 5100 Cortex-X925/X4/A720 SoC, 7.2 TOPS Genio 420 Cortex-A78/A55 SoC for AIoT applications
summary: MediaTek has unveiled two new SoCs, the Genio Pro 5100 and Genio 420, designed for AIoT applications. The Genio Pro 5100 features a 3nm all big-core architecture and 50+ TOPS NPU, supporting Edge AI. The Genio 420 is a cost-efficient 6nm platform for smart home and industrial IoT devices.
insight: Advancements in AIoT capabilities drive growth in smart home and industrial applications.

title: Qualcomm X105 6G-ready 5G modem to deliver 14.8 Gbps download and 4.2 Gbps upload peak data speeds
summary: Qualcomm X105 is a 5G modem that supports peak data speeds of 14.8 Gbps download and 4.2 Gbps upload. It's also 6G-ready and features a 6nm RF transceiver, quad-band GNSS, and an AI processor for optimized data traffic. The modem enables development and testing for 6G connectivity.
insight: Advancements in 5G modems pave way for 6G breakthroughs

title: Qualcomm s Wear Elite diagram shows sensors at chip's heart
summary: The Snapdragon Wear Elite chip supports up to 50 sensors, enhancing wearable device capabilities.
insight: Increased sensor capacity enables more accurate and comprehensive health monitoring.

title: DR-DOS is coming back from the dead, rebuilt from scratch in pure assembly
summary: DR-DOS is revived as a text-based operating system rebuilt in pure assembly. Whitehorn Ltd. Co. is behind the revival, carrying forward Gary Kildall's legacy. The new DR-DOS has a dedicated website.
insight: Preservation of vintage computing heritage through modern development efforts

title: Oracle prepares new round of layoffs while doubling down on AI infrastructure
summary: Oracle plans to spend $1.1 billion on layoffs, having spent $982 million so far. The fund is aimed at restructuring the company's infrastructure with AI. This move has raised concerns about the human cost of the restructuring.
insight: Tech giants' push for AI may come at significant human cost.

title: Game devs cash in: Nearly 6,000 Steam games earned over $100,000 last year
summary: Over 6,000 Steam games earned over $100,000 in 2023, surpassing pre-pandemic numbers. This indicates a sustained growth in the gaming industry. The number of high-earning titles has more than doubled since 2020.
insight: Steam's popularity and growth drive increased revenue for game developers.

title: Samsung's glasses-free 3D gaming monitor is getting more games
summary: Samsung expands gaming content for its Odyssey 3D monitor ecosystem at GDC 2026, enhancing the 3D gaming experience.
insight: More games means greater adoption of 3D gaming technology.

title: Valve says its loot boxes are more like trading cards than gambling
summary: Valve defends its loot boxes, claiming they're like trading cards. New York Attorney General Letitia James sued Valve in February, arguing that mystery boxes in popular games constitute 'quintessential gambling'.
insight: Monetary value of cosmetics blurs line between games and gambling.

title: It's come to this: V-Color to launch 1+1 DDR5 value pack with one RAM stick and one filler module
summary: V-Color is launching a value pack with one RAM stick and one filler module, an unusual approach to fake memory kits.
insight: Unconventional packaging may appeal to budget-conscious consumers seeking cost-effective solutions.

title: Valve details performance targets for Steam Machine and Steam Frame
summary: Valve has specified performance targets for Steam Machine and Steam Frame. The Steam Machine will have more relaxed verification requirements due to its expected performance headroom, while the Steam Frame will have stricter requirements for locally installed games.
insight: Different performance expectations for Steam Machine and Steam Frame applications

title: Fortnite "Save the World" mode will return as a free-to-play game in 2026
summary: Fortnite's 'Save the World' mode returns as a free-to-play game in 2026. Originally released in 2017, it's a PvE action-building title with base construction and combat against AI enemies. The mode was built before the popular battle royale mode.
insight: Fortnite reclaims its roots with a nostalgic PvE mode revival

title: Hisense TVs caught showing non-skippable ads when changing inputs or channels
summary: Hisense TVs, including Akai and Loewe models, show non-skippable ads when changing inputs or channels, a behavior that has persisted for years.
insight: Consumer frustration and loyalty concerns due to intrusive advertising

title: Windows 11 "Xbox Mode" UI coming to all PCs in April
summary: Windows' full-screen 'Xbox Mode' UI is exiting beta in April, available on all PCs, enabling users to launch games and apps with a controller.
insight: Enhanced gaming experience for PC users with Xbox controller support.

title: These Wall-E-like robots are making Texas sidewalks safer
summary: Residents in Burleson, Texas, are seeing Wall-E-like robots, called Daxbots, making their sidewalks safer. These robots are designed to help with maintenance and upkeep. They are the first of their kind to be used in a neighborhood setting.
insight: Automated maintenance can improve community safety and reduce maintenance costs.

title: OS/2 isn't dead: ArcaOS update brings the 1980s-era OS to modern UEFI PCs
summary: A legacy OS, OS/2, has been updated to run on modern PCs under the name ArcaOS. This brings 1980s-era technology to UEFI PCs, allowing it to coexist with modern operating systems. The update is a result of ongoing development by Arca Noae, the sole current licensor of OS/2 technology.
insight: Legacy OS innovation continues despite modern ecosystem advancements.

title: YouTube expands unskippable 30-second ads to TVs after $40 billion revenue year
summary: YouTube is expanding unskippable 30-second ads to its TV app, mimicking legacy TV spots.
insight: YouTube shifts TV app strategy to traditional linear-style advertising model.

title: Google brings new PC-focused features to the Play Store, including "play anywhere"
summary: In a developer post titled "Expanding our stage for PC and paid titles," Google revealed a slew of new features many of them PC-focused coming to the Play Store. Read Entire Article

title: DLSS vs FSR vs XeSS: Game Support Across 650+ Titles
summary: A comparison of DLSS, FSR, and XeSS support across 650+ PC games reveals the adoption rates of these upscaling and frame generation technologies. Nvidia's DLSS leads the pack, followed by AMD's FSR and Intel's XeSS. The study highlights the varying levels of support from game developers.
insight: Nvidia's DLSS dominates upscaling adoption among PC game developers.
